skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: A fish hatchery uses three tanks for holding fish before release. Tank A holds 12 fish, each weighing less than 6 ounces. Tank B holds 10 fish, each weighing at least 6 ounces but no more than 15 ounces. Tank C holds 11 fish, each weighing more than 15 ounces. Which of the following could be the median of the weights, in ounces, of these 33 fish?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. With 33 fish ordered by weight, the median is the 17th value. Positions 1–12 are from Tank A (< 6 oz), positions 13–22 are from Tank B ([6, 15] oz), and positions 23–33 are from Tank C (> 15 oz). The 17th value falls in Tank B, so the median is between 6 and 15 ounces. Only 9 lies in this interval.

Choices A and C are below 6. Choice B is above 15.
